mapping.a_element: spin-r/2 vertex model with domain-wall boundary conditions (statistical-mechanical setting)
integer parameter r (spin / polytope dilation factor)
mapping.b_element: integer parameter N (matrix rank / large-N expansion parameter)

brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is exact enumerative combinatorics via convex geometry — the objects are the lattice points of the r-th dilate of an integral polytope, counted exactly by an Ehrhart polynomial in r — whereas Paper B's machinery is the asymptotic 1/N expansion of integrals over the unitary group (single-link integral, saddle-point/external-field solutions, double-scaling limit). Both borrow statistical-mechanics vocabulary (“spin,” “matrix”), but the parameters r and N and the underlying processes (finite lattice-point enumeration vs. large-N asymptotics of group integrals) play entirely different roles, so no role-for-role correspondence of the driving machinery holds.
